940 /** 941 * Method that allows checking whether this node is JSON Array node 942 * and contains a value for specified index 943 * If this is the case 944 * (including case of specified indexing having null as value), returns true; 945 * otherwise returns false. 946 *

947 * Note: array element indexes are 0-based. 948 *

949 * This method is equivalent to: 950 *

   951	     *   node.get(index) != null
   952	     *
953 *

954 * NOTE: this method will return true for explicitly added 955 * null values. 956 * 957 * @param index Index to check 958 * 959 * @return True if this node is a JSON Object node, and has a property 960 * entry with specified name (with any value, including null value) 961 */ 962 public boolean has(int index) { 963 return get(index) != null; 964 } 965 966 /** 967 * Method that is similar to {@link #has(String)}, but that will 968 * return false for explicitly added nulls. 969 *

970 * This method is functionally equivalent to: 971 *

   972	     *   node.get(fieldName) != null && !node.get(fieldName).isNull()
   973	     *
974 * 975 * @since 2.1 976 */ 977 public boolean hasNonNull(String fieldName) { 978 JsonNode n = get(fieldName); 979 return (n != null) && !n.isNull(); 980 } 981 982 /** 983 * Method that is similar to {@link #has(int)}, but that will 984 * return false for explicitly added nulls. 985 *

986 * This method is equivalent to: 987 *

   988	     *   node.get(index) != null && !node.get(index).isNull()
   989	     *
990 * 991 * @since 2.1 992 */ 993 public boolean hasNonNull(int index) { 994 JsonNode n = get(index); 995 return (n != null) && !n.isNull(); 996 } 997 998 /* 999 /********************************************************** 1000 /* Public API, container access
